result:
Only the intended file is new. The experiment is pre-registered.

## Summary

This iteration pivots away from the field/classification mechanism that's gone unquestioned since round 16 (twenty rounds of only tuning satellite modules — samplers, gates, margin losses — around it), per the mandatory pivot and the plateau diagnosis in `champion-failure-mode-probes` (true cell ranks top-1 only ~2% of the time despite being in the top-20-of-1024 33–58% of the time).

**Design:** Replace the 1024-cell classification field + soft-argmax decode with an 8-hypothesis regression head (fixed anchor points + learned offsets, softmax weights), trained with winner-take-all loss (nearest hypothesis gets the gradient, others get an ε=0.05 trickle) plus an 8-way cross-entropy on the weight head — shrinking the ranking problem from 1-of-1024 to 1-of-8. The 4 rotation views each pick their own winning hypothesis; confidence comes from cross-view agreement instead of field-shape statistics. I flagged the real risk explicitly in `expected_outcome`: plain point regression is exactly what round 2 beat with the field approach (shrinkage-to-mean on ambiguous crops), so this is a bet that 8 *learned, competing* hypotheses avoid that collapse — and a regression to round-1/2-era error would be informative in itself, not just a failure.

Wrote `runs/pending_experiment.json` with hypothesis/method/expected_outcome, a figcheck-passing architecture SVG (reused the unchanged camera-frame/rotation-fan/trunk/layout stages verbatim from round 36, redrew everything from the head onward), and a step-by-step implementation brief for `model/model.py` and the mechanical `GRID_K` decoupling needed in `model/train.py`. Ready for the harness to hand off to the implementation agent.

tool_input.command:
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=36;" > /tmp/exp36.svg
wc -c /tmp/exp36.svg
head -c 3000 /tmp/exp36.svg
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=36;" > /tmp/exp36.svg && wc -c /tmp/exp36.svg && head -c 3000 /tmp/exp36.svg
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=36;" > /tmp/exp36.svg

cat > /workspace/low-light-geolocalization-autoresearch/runs/_scratch/gen_svg.py << 'PYEOF'
import sqlite3, re, sys

conn = sqlite3.connect("experiments.sqlite")
svg36 = conn.execute("SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=36").fetchone()[0]
conn.close()

cut_gate = svg36.index("<text x='515' y='40'")
cut_trainlane = svg36.index("<line x1='20' y1='222'")
head = svg36[:cut_gate]
trainlane = svg36[cut_trainlane:]

INK, MUT, FAINT, ACC, OCH = "#111111", "#6b6a60", "#9b998c", "#8c2f1f", "#8a6a1e"

# reuse the exact old fan lines (479->558) verbatim
fan_start = svg36.index("<line x1='479' y1='94.4'")
fan_end = svg36.index("<rect x='560'")
fan_lines = svg36[fan_start:fan_end]

anchors = [(574.4,98.4),(596,98.4),(617.6,98.4),
           (574.4,120),            (617.6,120),
           (574.4,141.6),(596,141.6),(617.6,141.6)]
winner_base = (617.6,141.6)
anchors_faint = [a for a in anchors if a != winner_base]
jitter = [(-2,-2),(2,-1.4),(-1,2.6),(1.6,1)]
winner_dots = [(winner_base[0]+dx, winner_base[1]+dy) for dx,dy in jitter]

mid = []
mid.append(fan_lines)
mid.append("<rect x='560' y='84' width='72' height='72' fill='none' stroke='#111111' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/>")
for (ax,ay) in anchors_faint:
    mid.append(f"<circle cx='{ax:.1f}' cy='{ay:.1f}' r='2' fill='{INK}' opacity='.4'/>")
for (dx_,dy_) in winner_dots:
    mid.append(f"<circle cx='{dx_:.1f}' cy='{dy_:.1f}' r='2.2' fill='{ACC}' fill-opacity='.8' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='.8'/>")
mid.append(f"<text x='596' y='172' font-size='10.5' fill='{ACC}' font-weight='600' text-anchor='middle'>Multi-hypothesis head</text>")
mid.append(f"<text x='596' y='184' font-size='9.5' fill='{MUT}' text-anchor='middle'>8 learned points, softmax weight</text>")
mid.append(f"<text x='596' y='196' font-size='9' fill='{FAINT}' font-style='italic' text-anchor='middle'>brightness folded in as input, no dispatcher</text>")

tx, ty = 812, 120
for (dx_,dy_) in winner_dots:
    mid.append(f"<line x1='{dx_:.1f}' y1='{dy_:.1f}' x2='{tx}' y2='{ty}' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='.8' opacity='.55'/>")
mid.append(f"<line x1='{tx-7}' y1='{ty}' x2='{tx+7}' y2='{ty}' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='1.4'/>")
mid.append(f"<line x1='{tx}' y1='{ty-7}' x2='{tx}' y2='{ty+7}' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='1.4'/>")
mid.append(f"<circle cx='{tx}' cy='{ty}' r='3.9' fill='none' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='1.4'/>")
mid.append(f"<circle cx='{tx}' cy='{ty}' r='1.5' fill='{ACC}'/>")
mid.append(f"<text x='812' y='150' font-size='10.5' fill='{ACC}' font-weight='600' text-anchor='middle'>4-view weighted vote</text>")
mid.append(f"<text x='812' y='164' font-size='9.5' fill='{MUT}' text-anchor='middle'>weight-argmax point per heading, softmax-blended</text>")
mid.append(f"<text x='812' y='178' font-size='9.5' fill='{MUT}' text-anchor='middle'>conf: 4-view agreement × winning weight</text>")
mid.append("<text id='frozen-output' x='828' y='112' font-size='11' fill='#9b998c' font-weight='600' text-anchor='start'>(lat, lon, confidence)</text>")
mid.append("<text x='828' y='126' font-size='9' fill='#9b998c' font-style='italic' text-anchor='start'>frozen contract</text>")

middle = "".join(mid)

# --- training lane surgery ---
idx_block2 = trainlane.index("<text x='430' y='326'")
trainlane_head = trainlane[:idx_block2]

new_train_tail = []
new_train_tail.append(f"<text x='430' y='326' font-size='9.5' fill='{ACC}' font-weight='600' text-anchor='start'>+ winner-take-all: nearest of 8 hyps gets the L2 grad, others ε=0.05 (NEW)</text>")
new_train_tail.append(f"<text x='430' y='340' font-size='9.5' fill='{ACC}' text-anchor='start'>weight head trained by cross-entropy vs. the nearest-hypothesis index (8-way, not 1024-way)</text>")

# small WTA glyph: a target dot with several candidate dots at various
# distances; nearest one bold-connected (full loss), farther ones
# faint-dashed (epsilon trickle)
gx0, gy0 = 280, 250
new_train_tail.append(f"<rect x='{gx0}' y='{gy0}' width='78' height='78' fill='none' stroke='{INK}' stroke-width='1.2'/>")
true_pt = (gx0+27, gy0+50)
cands = [(gx0+34, gy0+43), (gx0+58, gy0+18), (gx0+12, gy0+14), (gx0+63, gy0+62)]
new_train_tail.append(f"<circle cx='{true_pt[0]}' cy='{true_pt[1]}' r='3' fill='{INK}'/>")
for i, (cx_, cy_) in enumerate(cands):
    nearest = (i == 0)
    color = ACC if nearest else INK
    dash = "" if nearest else " stroke-dasharray='2 3'"
    op = ".9" if nearest else ".35"
    lw = "1.4" if nearest else "0.8"
    new_train_tail.append(f"<line x1='{true_pt[0]}' y1='{true_pt[1]}' x2='{cx_}' y2='{cy_}' stroke='{color}' stroke-width='{lw}' opacity='{op}'{dash}/>")
    new_train_tail.append(f"<circle cx='{cx_}' cy='{cy_}' r='{3 if nearest else 2}' fill='{color}' fill-opacity='{0.8 if nearest else 0.4}'/>")
new_train_tail.append(f"<text x='319' y='344' font-size='10.5' fill='{ACC}' font-weight='600' text-anchor='middle'>winner-take-all regression</text>")
new_train_tail.append(f"<text x='319' y='356' font-size='9.5' fill='{ACC}' text-anchor='middle'>nearest hypothesis (bold) wins the gradient; the rest get ε=0.05</text>")
new_train_tail.append(f"<path d='M364,289 H420 V326' stroke='{ACC}' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 3' fill='none' opacity='.7'/>")
new_train_tail.append(f"<path d='M 420,322 L 426,326 L 420,330 Z' fill='{ACC}' opacity='.7'/>")
new_train_tail.append("</svg>")

new_trainlane = trainlane_head + "".join(new_train_tail)

full_svg = head + middle + new_trainlane
Path = None
with open("runs/_scratch/new_arch.svg", "w") as f:
    f.write(full_svg)
print(len(full_svg), "bytes written")
PYEOF
.venv/bin/python runs/_scratch/gen_svg.py 2>&1 | tail -30 || python3 runs/_scratch/gen_svg.py 2>&1 | tail -30

python3 - << 'EOF'
import re
svg = open("/workspace/low-light-geolocalization-autoresearch/runs/_scratch/new_arch.svg").read()
print(len(svg))
print(re.search(r"viewBox='[^']*'", svg).group(0))
print("frozen-input count:", svg.count("frozen-input"))
print("frozen-output count:", svg.count("frozen-output"))
print("cam-terrain count:", svg.count("cam-terrain"))
EOF
